sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Charcoal moved right again to cols 50-53. The yellow strip is at cols 54-55. If I click the top blue square one more time, the charcoal should move to cols 54-57, overlapping with the yellow strip at cols 54-55.
Let me track the charcoal positions:
- Initial: cols 46-49
- After click on top blue: cols 50-53
- After click on bottom blue: cols 46-49 (back)
- After click on top blue again: cols 50-53
So each click on top blue moves right 4, each click on bottom blue moves left 4.
Current: cols 50-53
Next click on top blue: cols 54-57 (would overlap with yellow at 54-55)
Let me try clicking the top blue square again to see what happens when the charcoal overlaps with the yellow strip.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Charcoal at cols 50-53, yellow strip at cols 54-55. One more right move will overlap them. Top blue = move right 4, bottom blue = move left 4.
Plan: Click top blue square to move charcoal onto yellow strip and observe what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click top blue square to move charcoal onto yellow strip
print("Clicking top blue to move charcoal onto yellow strip")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 62}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("\nAfter click:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'Y', 'P']:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print(f"\nCharcoal area (rows 44-50, cols 48-58):")
for r in range(44, 51):
    print(f"  {r}: '{ascii_lines[r][48:58]}'")

# Check if any objects disappeared or changed
print("\nAll objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
